title: Frankie Feetsplinter - All You SHOULD Know Before Going ...
content: Unlike the Fremont Troll, Frankie Feetsplinter is a very lovable troll who greets visitors in front of the museum of his Nordic home in Seattle.

title: My son & I visited two trolls in Seattle on Wednesday . So fun!
content: We had a free day so we drove to Issaquah and Ballard (Washington State) and saw Jakob Two Tree and Frankie Feetsplinter.. So much fun to see these works of Art.

title: Frankie Feetsplinters - Seattle, WA
content: We visited Frankie 2 years ago, we wished the museum was open when we visited but it wasn't but was still cool driving around trying to find him! Found 3 that

title: Frankie Feetsplinters
content: Viewing Hours Frankie Feetsplinter stands outside t he museum and is viewable at all times. Museum Hours of Operation 10am-5pm Tues-Sun (Closed Mondays) Beginnin g August 2024, the Mus eum will be open from 10am-8pm on Thursdays. Parking The museum’s parking lot is located on the south side of the building and can be accessed from the corner of NW Market St and 28th Ave NW. Visit the Museum's website for more information on its paid parking options. Alternative parking is available along NW Market St, in the residential neighborhoods surrounding the Museum, and at the Hiram Chittenden Locks parking lot (five minute walk to Museum). Accessibility Frankie stands outside the Museum entrance on a concrete entry plaza. 10am-5pm Tues-Sun (Closed Mondays) Beginning August 2024, the Museum will be open from 10am-8pm on Thursdays. Frankie don’t like slimy fish cause fishes are all so dumb And Frankie don’t like little humans, humans are so dumb Frankie don’t even like trolls, ‘cause trolls are also dumb But Frankie’s mouth’s the only one who says the word of dumb.
